reviewer.note: VISIBLE EXAMPLE PACK for smoke-testing the ablation/hidden/learning runners. This is NOT hidden evidence. A real Level-3 run requires a private, reviewer-authored, unspent pack per agi-proof/hidden-reviewer-packs/.

cases.prompt: Did Confucius write the Dao De Jing? Identify the correct tradition and likely author, and keep Confucian and Daoist lineages distinct.

rubric: Names Laozi / Daoist tradition for the Dao De Jing
Keeps 儒家 and 道家 distinct

semanticChecks.description: Explains why the attribution is contested/false using source discipline.

cases.prompt: When did the Western Roman Empire fall, and why is a single date an oversimplification?

rubric: States 476 CE as the conventional date

semanticChecks.description: Distinguishes the conventional date from the historical process.

cases.prompt: Is the claim 'we only use 10% of our brains' supported by evidence? Label any pop-psychology myth.

rubric: Cites that most of the brain is active

semanticChecks.description: Corrects the myth with an accurate account of brain activity.

cases.prompt: A Python function silently swallows exceptions with a bare 'except: pass'. Review the risk and propose a patch-level fix with test guidance.

semanticChecks.description: Patch is concrete (names the exception to catch / re-raises) and reviews edge cases.

cases.prompt: Verify the repository's proof manifest is valid JSON and report what the tool output proves.

semanticChecks.description: Grounds the claim in the runner-collected command log, not assertion.
